narrative: OMX30 opens in a technically ambiguous bull regime: +7.7% above the 200-day MA but only -0.1% from the 50-day, with RSI at 41 showing neutral positioning and MACD below signal. The ADX at 13.7 flags weak trend conviction, triggering a 30% confidence reduction per protocol. However, European peers rallied solidly overnight (DAX +141bp, CAC +53bp) and the weighted correlation signal is constructive at +0.639%. Copper strength (+135bp) is a tailwind for Boliden and materials, while the -63bp drop in US 10Y yields removes some tech DCF headwind. Key risk: MACD remains bearish, RSI is nowhere near reversal extremes, and geopolitical headlines (Iran-Israel tensions) could reignite risk-off behavior despite this morning's positive tone.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens modestly higher on ripple from European strength and commodity tailwinds, targeting 3135–3155 range. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B) and mining (Boliden) get a bid from copper and lower refinancing costs, but conviction is LOW given weak ADX (13.7) and MACD divergence—treat any bounce as a sell opportunity into resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Banks are mildly positive on yield stability; avoid overweighting rate-sensitive real estate until we break above 3150. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for any shock that could derail the morning's fragile bounce. Key support sits at 3085 (lower Bollinger); a break below invalidates the bull setup. Geopolitical risk (Iran-Israel) is live—any escalation will spike VIX and flush momentum trades.
drivers: Strong European equity momentum (DAX +1.41%, CAC +0.53%) with weighted correlation signal +0.639%
Copper +1.35% and firm commodity backdrop supports industrial/mining complex
Yield decline (-63bp on US 10Y) reduces refinancing pressure; OMX30 +7.7% above 200-MA sustains bull regime

sectorImpacts.reason: Copper +1.35% and Brent -5.29% (lower input costs) provide mild tailwind. DAX +1.41% sentiment supports capital goods demand outlook.
Copper +1.35% is a direct revenue driver; gold stable at 4604. Boliden is primary beneficiary.
US 10Y yield -63bp to 4.39% slightly reduces NIM expansion narrative, but Swedish rates likely stabilize. No material tailwind or headwind.
Yield decline is mildly positive for discount rates, but low conviction in broader market direction limits upside.
NASDAQ +0.89% provides mild support; yield decline reduces DCF pressure. Weak ADX caps upside enthusiasm.
No risk-off signal evident (VIX down to 16.89), so defensive rotation not triggered. Maintain neutral stance.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.35% and gold stable—direct revenue drivers. Monitor intraday above 3135 for entry.
USD/SEK weak at 9.228 (-0.82%) limits FX tailwind, but DAX strength and industrial sector positive bias support peer trading.
Export-sensitive; weak USD/SEK is a drag, but DAX +1.41% and copper strength offset. Monitor for breakout above 3150.
NASDAQ +0.89% provides mild support, but MACD bearish divergence on OMX30 may weigh. Hold until technical clarity.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (market sensitive). No major Swedish economic releases. Monitor ECB speak for any yield curve commentary.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel tensions per FT headline) could trigger VIX spike and unwind risk rally. Weak ADX and MACD divergence mean momentum is fragile—gap fills and reversal patterns are elevated risk. Trade positioning could be long and vulnerable.
technicalSummary: ADX 13.7 (weak trend, reduce conviction 30% per protocol)
Stochastic K=31.5 D=17.4 (early bounce signal but not confirmation yet)
52-week positioning: 80% of range, -5.0% from high (some mean reversion risk)
